claim: Agent harnesses are orchestration layers that manage model selection, tool allocation, and execution loops for multi-step tasks
actionable: Design agent systems with a clear harness layer that routes subtasks to appropriate models (e.g., use smaller models for simple steps)
source_title: What is an Agent Harness? and How to build a great one!

claim: Agent harnesses enable delegation by allowing high-level models to spawn lower-cost models for specific tool use
actionable: In your harness, detect when a subtask can be delegated (e.g., code generation, API calls), and route to cheaper/faster models via sub-agent pattern
source_title: What is an Agent Harness? and How to build a great one!

claim: Error handling and retry logic in agent harnesses should be context-aware to avoid repeating failed patterns
actionable: When an agent tool fails, provide the error to the model with relevant context and allow strategy adjustment before retry
source_title: What is an Agent Harness? and How to build a great one!

claim: Token budgeting is crucial in agent harnesses—allocate token pools per subtask to prevent runaway costs
actionable: Define max_tokens per agent step and implement circuit breakers when a step approaches its token limit
source_title: What is an Agent Harness? and How to build a great one!
